 {"id":"bd-15n","title":"Update --continue flag to use SQLite index","description":"# Update --continue flag to use SQLite index

## Goal
Use SQLite index for fast lookup of most recent session when --continue is used.

## Current Implementation (src/main.rs)
Currently scans filesystem for most recent:
```rust
if args.continue_session {
    let session_dir = sessions_dir_for_cwd(&cwd);
    // Scans all files, sorts by mtime
    let most_recent = fs::read_dir(&session_dir)?
        .filter_map(|e| e.ok())
        .max_by_key(|e| e.metadata().ok()?.modified().ok()?);
    // ...
}
```

## New Implementation
```rust
if args.continue_session {
    let index = SessionIndex::open_default()?;
    let cwd = std::env::current_dir()?.to_string_lossy().to_string();
    
    if let Some(meta) = index.find_recent(&cwd)? {
        return load_session(&meta.path);
    }
    
    // Fallback to filesystem
    // ...
}
```

## Benefits
- O(1) lookup instead of O(n) filesystem scan
- Works across hundreds of sessions instantly
- Consistent with session picker behavior

## Dependencies
- bd-3nz (indexing must work first)

## Testing
- Test: --continue uses index
- Test: --continue falls back to filesystem
- Test: Performance improved

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] --continue queries index
- [ ] Fallback works if index empty
- [ ] Faster for users with many sessions","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss

 {"id":"bd-167l","title":"Bench: JSONL schema + env fingerprint for extension benchmarks","description":"# Goal
Define the canonical, machine-readable output format for extension benchmark runs.

# Scope / Deliverables
- JSONL record types (per run, per extension, per scenario)
- Environment fingerprint fields:
  - OS/kernel
  - CPU model + core count
  - build profile (debug/release)
  - git commit hash
  - feature flags (ext-conformance, wasm, etc.)
- Timing stats fields:
  - raw samples OR a histogram
  - p50/p95/p99
  - warmup count
- Deterministic formatting + stable key ordering.

# Why
Everything downstream (CI gates, trend tracking, baseline comparisons, docs) depends on this being stable.

# Acceptance
- One end-to-end run emits valid JSONL that validates against the schema.
- Stable ordering across repeated deterministic runs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:01:47.176631505Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T01:32:30.828204355Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T01:32:30.828039217Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bench","extensions","perf"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-167l","depends_on_id":"bd-20s9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:01:47.330087273Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1696","title":"Create standard event payload fixtures for both runtimes","description":"# Create standard event payload fixtures for both runtimes

## Context
Extensions subscribe to events via pi.on("event_name", handler). For conformance testing, we need to FIRE the same events in both runtimes and compare handler responses.

## Events Defined in pi-mono types.ts
From the ExtensionEvent type union:
- tool_call: {tool, input} -> {allow, message, replace_input}
- tool_result: {tool, result} -> {result} (can modify)
- turn_start: {} -> void
- turn_end: {} -> void
- before_agent_start: {} -> {message, system_prompt}
- input: {content, source} -> {content, skip}
- context: {messages, usage} -> {system_prompt, messages}
- resources_discover: {resources} -> {resources}
- user_bash: {command, result} -> {modified_result}
- session_before_compact: {preparation} -> {should_compact, custom_messages}
- session_before_tree: {options} -> {should_show_tree}

## What To Do
1. For each event type, create 2-3 standard payloads (normal case + edge cases)
2. Save as tests/ext_conformance/fixtures/event_payloads.json
3. Document expected behavior for each event type
4. Include payloads that test: normal flow, blocking behavior, error handling, empty handlers

## Fixture Format
{
  "event_payloads": {
    "tool_call": [
      {
        "name": "basic_tool_call",
        "payload": {"tool": "bash", "input": {"command": "echo hi"}},
        "default_response": {"allow": true}
      },
      {
        "name": "blocked_tool_call",
        "payload": {"tool": "rm", "input": {"path": "/etc/passwd"}},
        "default_response": {"allow": false, "message": "blocked"}
      }
    ]
  }
}

## Acceptance Criteria
- All event types from ExtensionEvent have at least 2 payloads
- Payloads are valid JSON matching the TypeScript type definitions
- Edge cases included (empty arrays, null values, unicode, large payloads)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:20:34.169239280Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T17:02:20.870081762Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T17:02:20.870011520Z","close_reason":"Added missing event types (model_select, session_start/switch/fork/compact/tree) and ensured 2+ payloads each; added second session_shutdown payload","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1696","depends_on_id":"bd-6koq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:20:34.185490611Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-16kl","title":"Unit: session persistence corruption + edge cases (no mocks)","description":"# Goal
Cover session persistence edge cases in `src/session.rs` without mocks, focusing on corruption recovery and branching semantics.

# Why / User Impact
Session files are the backbone of continuity. Corruption recovery, atomic writes, and branch metadata must be robust so users never lose work.

# Scope (Granular)
- Corrupted JSONL lines: verify they are skipped, warnings emitted, and remaining entries load.
- Leaf selection: ensure `leaf_id` points to last valid entry after corruption.
- Branch summaries + compaction summaries: verify serialization, render paths, and round‑trip invariants.
- Session save: verify atomic write behavior and deterministic naming under temp dirs.
- Export path handling: invalid path / permission denied surfaces helpful errors.

# Logging / Artifacts
- Use TestHarness/TestLogger; capture temp file paths and stderr warnings.
- Record corrupted JSONL fixtures and any exported files as artifacts.

# Acceptance Criteria
- Tests in `tests/session_conformance.rs` or a new `tests/session_persistence.rs`.
- No mocks; real filesystem temp dirs only.
- Deterministic, race‑free assertions (avoid wall‑clock coupling).
- Explicit assertions on warning text and recovered message count.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss

 {"id":"bd-16uv","title":"Perf: eliminate per-chunk allocations in SSE stream (SseStream)","description":"# Goal
Reduce CPU + allocation churn on provider streaming path by removing per-chunk String/Vec allocations in src/sse.rs (SseStream UTF-8 handling).

# Why
SSE streaming is on the hot path for all providers. Today SseStream copies bytes into utf8_buffer, then allocates an owned String for valid UTF-8, then copies again into SseParser\x27s buffer. It also allocates a new Vec for trailing bytes when UTF-8 is split across chunks.

# Approach (Extreme Optimization)
- Baseline: add a micro-bench covering SseStream parsing with realistic chunk sizes.
- Optimize: feed &str slices directly from utf8_buffer into SseParser (no intermediate String) and keep trailing bytes in-place (no to_vec).
- Prove: existing SSE unit tests + added chunked UTF-8 regression coverage.

# Acceptance
- [ ] No behavior change (all SSE tests pass)
- [ ] Micro-bench shows improved throughput for chunked inputs
- [ ] Quality gates pass","notes":"## Implementation
- `src/sse.rs`: add a fast-path in `SseStream::poll_next_event` to avoid copying the full incoming chunk into `utf8_buffer` when there is no pending UTF-8 tail.
- Keeps existing behavior for invalid UTF-8 (surface `InvalidData`) and for partial UTF-8 sequences (buffer remainder, continue parsing next chunk).

## Measurement (Criterion)
Command:
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/pi_agent_rust_target_blackfalcon cargo bench --bench tools sse_stream -- --noplot`

Before:
- `sse_stream/parse/64`:   `[883.52 us 889.96 us 895.67 us]`
- `sse_stream/parse/1024`: `[688.67 us 693.27 us 698.09 us]`
- `sse_stream/parse/4096`: `[692.80 us 698.86 us 704.64 us]`

After:
- `sse_stream/parse/64`:   `[841.65 us 850.27 us 858.67 us]` (mean ~-4.6% time; p<0.05)
- `sse_stream/parse/1024`: `[676.31 us 689.18 us 700.43 us]` (within noise threshold)
- `sse_stream/parse/4096`: `[682.95 us 688.83 us 698.19 us]` (no change detected)

## Isomorphism Proof
- Ordering preserved: underlying byte stream order is unchanged; only an intermediate copy is removed.
- Delimiters preserved: UTF-8 validation still gates feeding; the same bytes are fed into `SseParser` in the same sequence.
- Error behavior preserved: invalid UTF-8 still returns `InvalidData`.

## Gates
- `cargo fmt --check` OK
- `cargo check --all-targets` OK
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` OK
- `cargo test` OK","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BlackFalcon","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:07:46.482214999Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T11:01:42.589166154Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T11:01:42.589138733Z","close_reason":"Completed (SseStream fast-path + bench improvement + gates green)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-hzvc","title":"Task: Implement UiContext for TUI mode","description":"# Task: Implement UiContext for TUI Mode

## Objective

Create a TUI-based implementation of UiContext that integrates with the bubbletea model to show spinners, progress bars, and notifications.

## Background

The TUI uses bubbletea for interactive rendering. UI operations from extensions need to send Cmd messages to the model for rendering.

## Implementation

### File: src/tui/extension_ui.rs

```rust
use std::collections::HashMap;
use std::sync::{Arc, RwLock};
use uuid::Uuid;

use bubbles::{spinner::Spinner, progress::Progress};
use bubbletea::{Cmd, Model};

use crate::extensions::{UiContext, NotificationType, AskOptions, PromptType};
use crate::error::Result;

/// TUI implementation of UiContext.
/// 
/// Sends Cmd messages to the bubbletea model to render UI elements.
pub struct TuiUiContext {
    /// Channel to send commands to the TUI model
    cmd_tx: tokio::sync::mpsc::Sender<Cmd>,
    
    /// Active spinners (id -> message)
    spinners: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, String>>>,
    
    /// Active progress bars (id -> (current, total))
    progress_bars: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, (u64, u64)>>>,
}

impl TuiUiContext {
    pub fn new(cmd_tx: tokio::sync::mpsc::Sender<Cmd>) -> Self {
        Self {
            cmd_tx,
            spinners: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),
            progress_bars: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),
        }
    }
}

#[async_trait]
impl UiContext for TuiUiContext {
    fn show_spinner(&self, message: &str) -> Result<String> {
        let id = Uuid::new_v4().to_string();
        
        // Store spinner state
        self.spinners.write().unwrap().insert(id.clone(), message.to_string());
        
        // Send command to TUI
        let _ = self.cmd_tx.blocking_send(Cmd::Custom(Box::new(
            ExtensionUiCmd::ShowSpinner { id: id.clone(), message: message.to_string() }
        )));
        
        Ok(id)
    }
    
    fn update_spinner(&self, id: &str, message: &str) -> Result<()> {
        // Update stored state
        if let Some(msg) = self.spinners.write().unwrap().get_mut(id) {
            *msg = message.to_string();
        }
        
        // Send update command
        let _ = self.cmd_tx.blocking_send(Cmd::Custom(Box::new(
            ExtensionUiCmd::UpdateSpinner { id: id.to_string(), message: message.to_string() }
        )));
        
        Ok(())
    }
    
    fn stop_spinner(&self, id: &str) -> Result<()> {
        // Remove from state
        self.spinners.write().unwrap().remove(id);
        
        // Send stop command
        let _ = self.cmd_tx.blocking_send(Cmd::Custom(Box::new(
            ExtensionUiCmd::StopSpinner { id: id.to_string() }
        )));
        
        Ok(())
    }
    
    fn show_progress(&self, total: u64, message: Option<&str>) -> Result<String> {
        let id = Uuid::new_v4().to_string();
        
        // Store progress state
        self.progress_bars.write().unwrap().insert(id.clone(), (0, total));
        
        // Send command
        let _ = self.cmd_tx.blocking_send(Cmd::Custom(Box::new(
            ExtensionUiCmd::ShowProgress { 
                id: id.clone(), 
                total, 
                message: message.map(|s| s.to_string()),
            }
        )));
        
        Ok(id)
    }
    
    fn increment_progress(&self, id: &str, amount: u64) -> Result<()> {
        // Update stored state
        if let Some((current, total)) = self.progress_bars.write().unwrap().get_mut(id) {
            *current = (*current + amount).min(*total);
        }
        
        // Send increment command
        let _ = self.cmd_tx.blocking_send(Cmd::Custom(Box::new(
            ExtensionUiCmd::IncrementProgress { id: id.to_string(), amount }
        )));
        
        Ok(())
    }
    
    fn finish_progress(&self, id: &str) -> Result<()> {
        self.progress_bars.write().unwrap().remove(id);
        
        let _ = self.cmd_tx.blocking_send(Cmd::Custom(Box::new(
            ExtensionUiCmd::FinishProgress { id: id.to_string() }
        )));
        
        Ok(())
    }
    
    fn show_notification(&self, message: &str, notification_type: NotificationType) -> Result<()> {
        let _ = self.cmd_tx.blocking_send(Cmd::Custom(Box::new(
            ExtensionUiCmd::ShowNotification { 
                message: message.to_string(), 
                notification_type,
            }
        )));
        Ok(())
    }
    
    fn set_status_line(&self, text: &str) -> Result<()> {
        let _ = self.cmd_tx.blocking_send(Cmd::Custom(Box::new(
            ExtensionUiCmd::SetStatusLine { text: text.to_string() }
        )));
        Ok(())
    }
    
    fn clear_status_line(&self) -> Result<()> {
        let _ = self.cmd_tx.blocking_send(Cmd::Custom(Box::new(
            ExtensionUiCmd::ClearStatusLine
        )));
        Ok(())
    }
    
    async fn ask_user(&self, prompt: &str, options: AskOptions) -> Result<String> {
        // Create oneshot channel for response
        let (tx, rx) = tokio::sync::oneshot::channel();
        
        let _ = self.cmd_tx.send(Cmd::Custom(Box::new(
            ExtensionUiCmd::AskUser { 
                prompt: prompt.to_string(),
                options,
                response_tx: tx,
            }
        ))).await;
        
        // Wait for user response
        rx.await.map_err(|_| anyhow!("Prompt cancelled"))?
    }
}

/// Commands sent to TUI model for extension UI operations.
#[derive(Debug)]
pub enum ExtensionUiCmd {
    ShowSpinner { id: String, message: String },
    UpdateSpinner { id: String, message: String },
    StopSpinner { id: String },
    ShowProgress { id: String, total: u64, message: Option<String> },
    IncrementProgress { id: String, amount: u64 },
    FinishProgress { id: String },
    ShowNotification { message: String, notification_type: NotificationType },
    SetStatusLine { text: String },
    ClearStatusLine,
    AskUser { 
        prompt: String, 
        options: AskOptions,
        response_tx: tokio::sync::oneshot::Sender<Result<String>>,
    },
}
```

### TUI Model Integration

The TUI model needs to handle ExtensionUiCmd in its Update function:

```rust
impl Model for AppModel {
    fn update(&mut self, msg: Msg) -> Cmd {
        match msg {
            Msg::Custom(cmd) => {
                if let Some(ui_cmd) = cmd.downcast_ref::<ExtensionUiCmd>() {
                    self.handle_extension_ui(ui_cmd)
                } else {
                    Cmd::None
                }
            }
            // ... other messages
        }
    }
    
    fn handle_extension_ui(&mut self, cmd: &ExtensionUiCmd) -> Cmd {
        match cmd {
            ExtensionUiCmd::ShowSpinner { id, message } => {
                self.extension_spinners.insert(id.clone(), Spinner::new(message.clone()));
                Cmd::None
            }
            // ... other handlers
        }
    }
}
```

## Testing

1. Unit test: show_spinner returns unique ID
2. Unit test: update_spinner changes message
3. Unit test: stop_spinner removes spinner
4. Unit test: progress increments correctly
5. Integration test: TUI renders spinner
6. Integration test: TUI renders progress bar

## Dependencies

- Depends on: bd-3hw8 (UiContext trait)
- Part of: bd-2iag (UI Marshaling feature)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All operations send correct Cmd
- [ ] State tracking works
- [ ] TUI model handles commands

 {"id":"bd-ie1g","title":"Task: Implement execute_command() in PiJsRuntime","description":"# Task: Implement execute_command() in PiJsRuntime

## Objective

Add a method to execute an extension-registered slash command, passing arguments and returning the result.

## Background

When a user invokes an extension command (e.g., "/mycommand arg1 arg2"), we need to:
1. Call the JS handler with the command name and arguments
2. Wait for the async result
3. Parse and return the CommandResult

## Implementation

### JS Side

```javascript
globalThis.__pi_execute_command = async function(name, args) {
    const cmd = __pi_registry.commands.get(name);
    if (!cmd) {
        return JSON.stringify({ error: `Unknown command: ${name}` });
    }
    
    try {
        const result = await cmd.handler(args);
        return JSON.stringify({
            output: result?.output ?? null,
            continue_loop: result?.continueLoop ?? true,
            messages: result?.messages ?? null,
        });
    } catch (e) {
        return JSON.stringify({ error: e.message || String(e) });
    }
};
```

### Rust Side

```rust
impl PiJsRuntime {
    pub async fn execute_command(&self, name: &str, args: &str) -> Result<CommandResult> {
        let result = self.call_async_function(
            "__pi_execute_command",
            &[json!(name), json!(args)],
        ).await?;
        
        let result_json = result.as_str()
            .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!("Expected string"))?;
        let parsed: CommandResultRaw = serde_json::from_str(result_json)?;
        
        if let Some(error) = parsed.error {
            return Err(anyhow!("Command failed: {}", error));
        }
        
        Ok(CommandResult {
            output: parsed.output,
            continue_loop: parsed.continue_loop.unwrap_or(true),
            messages: parsed.messages,
        })
    }
}
```

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (6 cases)
1. test_valid_command_returns_result
2. test_unknown_command_returns_error
3. test_handler_throws_returns_error
4. test_output_field_passed_through
5. test_messages_field_parsed_correctly
6. test_continue_loop_defaults_to_true

### E2E Test Script (tests/e2e/slash_commands.sh)

```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

LOG_FILE="artifacts/slash_commands_e2e.jsonl"
mkdir -p artifacts

log() {
    echo "{\"timestamp\":\"$(date -Iseconds)\",\"event\":\"$1\",\"data\":$2}" >> "$LOG_FILE"
}

log "test_start" '{"suite":"slash_commands"}'

# Create test extension with commands
mkdir -p /tmp/test_cmd_ext
cat > /tmp/test_cmd_ext/index.js << 'EOF'
export function activate(ctx) {
    ctx.registerCommand({
        name: "greet",
        description: "Greet someone",
        usage: "/greet <name>",
        handler: async (args) => ({
            output: `Hello, ${args || 'World'}!`,
            continueLoop: true,
        }),
    });
    
    ctx.registerCommand({
        name: "exit",
        description: "Exit the agent",
        handler: async () => ({ continueLoop: false }),
    });
    
    ctx.registerCommand({
        name: "error",
        description: "Throws an error",
        handler: async () => { throw new Error("Intentional error"); },
    });
}
EOF

log "extension_created" '{"path":"/tmp/test_cmd_ext"}'

# Test command execution
echo "Testing /greet command..."
RESULT=$(pi --extension /tmp/test_cmd_ext --eval '/greet Claude' 2>&1)
log "greet_result" "{\"output\":\"$RESULT\"}"

echo "Testing /greet with no args..."
RESULT=$(pi --extension /tmp/test_cmd_ext --eval '/greet' 2>&1)
log "greet_no_args_result" "{\"output\":\"$RESULT\"}"

echo "Testing /error command..."
RESULT=$(pi --extension /tmp/test_cmd_ext --eval '/error' 2>&1) || true
log "error_result" "{\"output\":\"$RESULT\"}"

log "test_end" '{"status":"pass"}'

echo "All slash command E2E tests passed. Logs: $LOG_FILE"
```

- Depends on: bd-2qrn (get_registered_commands)
- Part of: bd-20vx (Slash Command Handler feature)
